evidence_map.evidence_quote: A Canadian reality TV show got me making my own miniatures
evidence_map.interpretation: Gaming-culture writer attributes hobby entry to a Canadian television format — implied buyer is a non-hobbyist arriving from mainstream media rather than the existing tabletop or dollhouse community.

unmapped.human_action: Monitor for a second independent signal within 60 days: beginner miniature search volume spike, craft-supply retailer SKU expansion into starter kits, second editorial mention from outside gaming media, or subreddit growth in r/minipainting or r/dollhouses.
unmapped.machine_action: Track aftermath.site and equivalent gaming-culture feeds for miniature crafting mentions; flag any craft-supply or toy retailer beginner kit launches; monitor Canadian broadcasting news for show renewal or US licensing deals.
unmapped.methodology_footer: Foreshore treats one-source proposals as watchlist material until independent source types confirm the movement. The 60-day falsification window is standard for low-confidence TV-driven hobby signals.
unmapped.uncertainty: Single source with no transaction, search volume, or demographic corroboration. The Canada-first craft adoption pattern is consistent historically but not deterministic. The specific Canadian show is unnamed in the source.
